title_en: Zakat, Fasting, and Hajj in Bada'i as-Sana'i
title_id: Zakat, Puasa, dan Haji dalam Bada'i as-Sana'i
content_en:
Al-Kasani's treatment of zakah, fasting, and hajj in Bada'i as-Sana'i applies his systematic method to the remaining three pillars of Islam, producing clear and navigable accounts of Hanafi positions on these foundational obligations.

For zakah, al-Kasani identifies the conditions that make zakah obligatory (shurut al-wujub) separately from the conditions for valid payment (shurut as-sihha). The conditions for obligation include: Islam, freedom, complete ownership, a nisab-level of wealth, and the passage of a full lunar year (hawl). He explains each condition with its evidential basis and practical implications, including the Hanafi position that the nisab need only be complete at the beginning and end of the hawl — temporary dips below the nisab during the year do not break the hawl in the Hanafi school (differing from other schools).

The categories of zakah-eligible wealth in Bada'i as-Sana'i are presented with al-Kasani's systematic analysis of the 'illah (effective cause) for each. Gold and silver are subject to zakah because they are monetary media (thaman) and measure of value. Trade goods are subject to zakah because they are held for profit through sale. Livestock are subject to zakah because they are self-proliferating productive wealth kept on open grazing. Agricultural produce is subject to zakah because it results from the productive capacity of the earth. These different 'illahs have different practical implications for which other types of wealth may be analogically included.

Fasting in Bada'i as-Sana'i presents the conditions for the obligation, the pillars of the fast, the invalidating acts, and the consequences of invalidation in al-Kasani's systematic format. The pillars of the fast are two: abstaining from everything that breaks the fast, and the intention. Al-Kasani explains the Hanafi position that the intention must be formed before midday, noting that this is based on the understanding that the 'day' of fasting begins at dawn and that intentions may be formed within the first half of the act being intended.

The invalidating acts (muftirat) of fasting are analyzed systematically in Bada'i as-Sana'i. Al-Kasani identifies which invalidating acts require both making up the day (qada') and expiation (kaffara) — intentional eating, drinking, and sexual intercourse during a Ramadan fast — and which require only making up the day. He also addresses the Hanafi school's distinctive positions on acts that break the fast: ear drops, nose drops, suppositories, cupping (the Hanafi school holds cupping does not break the fast), and other acts that involve substances entering the body through non-standard pathways.

Hajj in Bada'i as-Sana'i presents the Hanafi school's comprehensive framework for the pilgrimage. Al-Kasani identifies the conditions for the obligation of hajj, the pillars (arkan) — ihram, wuquf at Arafah, and tawaf al-ifadah — the necessary acts (wajibat) including sa'y, and the invalidating factors. His systematic organization of hajj law makes Bada'i as-Sana'i a particularly useful reference for understanding the distinction between pillars and necessary acts that is central to Hanafi hajj jurisprudence.
content_id:
Pembahasan al-Kasani tentang zakat, puasa, dan haji dalam Bada'i as-Sana'i menerapkan metode sistematisnya pada tiga rukun Islam yang tersisa, menghasilkan uraian yang jelas dan mudah dinavigasi tentang posisi-posisi Hanafi mengenai kewajiban-kewajiban fundamental ini.

Untuk zakat, al-Kasani mengidentifikasi syarat-syarat yang mewajibkan zakat (syurut al-wujub) secara terpisah dari syarat-syarat pembayaran yang sah (syurut as-sihhah). Syarat-syarat kewajiban meliputi: Islam, merdeka, kepemilikan penuh, kekayaan senilai nisab, dan berlalunya satu tahun hijriah penuh (haul). Ia menjelaskan setiap syarat beserta dasar dalil dan implikasi praktisnya, termasuk posisi Hanafi bahwa nisab hanya perlu lengkap di awal dan akhir haul — penurunan sementara di bawah nisab selama tahun berlangsung tidak memutus haul dalam mazhab Hanafi (berbeda dari mazhab-mazhab lain).

Kategori-kategori kekayaan yang wajib dizakati dalam Bada'i as-Sana'i disajikan dengan analisis sistematis al-Kasani tentang 'illah (sebab efektif) bagi masing-masing. Emas dan perak dikenai zakat karena merupakan media moneter (tsaman) dan ukuran nilai. Barang dagangan dikenai zakat karena dipegang untuk keuntungan melalui penjualan. Hewan ternak dikenai zakat karena merupakan kekayaan produktif yang berkembang sendiri yang dipelihara di padang penggembalaan terbuka. Hasil pertanian dikenai zakat karena merupakan hasil kapasitas produktif bumi. 'Illah-'illah yang berbeda ini memiliki implikasi praktis yang berbeda tentang jenis-jenis kekayaan lain yang dapat dimasukkan secara analogis.

Puasa dalam Bada'i as-Sana'i menyajikan syarat-syarat kewajiban, rukun-rukun puasa, perbuatan-perbuatan pembatal, dan konsekuensi pembatalan dalam format sistematis al-Kasani. Rukun puasa ada dua: menahan diri dari segala sesuatu yang membatalkan puasa, dan niat. Al-Kasani menjelaskan posisi Hanafi bahwa niat harus dilakukan sebelum tengah hari, mencatat bahwa hal ini didasarkan pada pemahaman bahwa 'hari' puasa dimulai dari fajar dan niat boleh dibentuk dalam paruh pertama dari tindakan yang diniatkan.

Perbuatan-perbuatan pembatal (muftirat) puasa dianalisis secara sistematis dalam Bada'i as-Sana'i. Al-Kasani mengidentifikasi perbuatan-perbuatan pembatal mana yang mengharuskan baik mengqada' hari itu maupun kafarat — makan, minum, dan berhubungan seksual dengan sengaja selama puasa Ramadan — dan mana yang hanya mengharuskan mengqada'. Ia juga membahas posisi-posisi khas mazhab Hanafi tentang perbuatan-perbuatan yang membatalkan puasa: tetes telinga, tetes hidung, supositoria, bekam (mazhab Hanafi berpendapat bekam tidak membatalkan puasa), dan perbuatan-perbuatan lain yang melibatkan zat-zat yang masuk ke tubuh melalui jalur yang tidak lazim.

Haji dalam Bada'i as-Sana'i menyajikan kerangka komprehensif mazhab Hanafi untuk ibadah haji. Al-Kasani mengidentifikasi syarat-syarat kewajiban haji, rukun-rukun (arkan) — ihram, wuquf di Arafah, dan tawaf ifadhah — perbuatan-perbuatan wajib (wajibat) termasuk sa'i, dan faktor-faktor pembatal. Organisasi sistematis al-Kasani atas hukum haji menjadikan Bada'i as-Sana'i referensi yang sangat berguna untuk memahami perbedaan antara rukun dan wajib yang menjadi pusat fikih haji Hanafi.
content_ar: Al-Kasani's treatment of الزكاة, الصيام, and الحج in Bada'i as-Sana'i applies his systematic method to the remaining three pillars of الإسلام, producing clear and navigable accounts of الحنفي positions on these foundational obligations. For الزكاة, al-Kasani identifies the conditions that make الزكاة obligatory (shurut al-wujub) separately from the conditions for valid payment (shurut as-sihha). The conditions for الالتزام include: الإسلام, freedom, complete ownership, a nisab-level of الثروة, and the passage of a full lunar year (hawl). He explains each condition with its evidential basis and practical implications, بما في ذلك the الحنفي position that the nisab need only be complete at the beginning and end of the hawl — temporary dips below the nisab خلال the year do not break the hawl in the الحنفي school (differing from other schools). The categories of الزكاة-eligible الثروة in Bada'i as-Sana'i are presented with al-Kasani's systematic analysis of the 'illah (effective cause) for each. Gold and silver are subject to الزكاة لأن they are monetary media (thaman) and measure of value. Trade goods are subject to الزكاة لأن they are held for profit through sale. Livestock are subject to الزكاة لأن they are self-proliferating productive الثروة kept on open grazing. Agricultural produce is subject to الزكاة لأن it results from the productive capacity of the earth. These different 'illahs have different practical implications for which other types of الثروة may be analogically included. الصيام in Bada'i as-Sana'i presents the conditions for the الالتزام, the pillars of the fast, the invalidating acts, and the consequences of invalidation in al-Kasani's systematic format. The pillars of the fast are two: abstaining from everything that breaks the fast, and the intention. Al-Kasani explains the الحنفي position that the intention must be formed قبل midday, noting that this is based on the الفهم that the 'day' of الصيام begins at dawn and that intentions may be formed within the أولاً half of the act being intended. The invalidating acts (muftirat) of الصيام are analyzed systematically in Bada'i as-Sana'i. Al-Kasani identifies which invalidating acts require both making up the day (القضاء') and expiation (kaffara) — intentional eating, drinking, and sexual intercourse خلال a Ramadan fast — and which require only making up the day. He أيضاً addresses the الحنفي school's distinctive positions on acts that break the fast: ear drops, nose drops, suppositories, cupping (the الحنفي school holds cupping does not break the fast), and other acts that involve substances entering the الجسد through non-standard pathways. الحج in Bada'i as-Sana'i presents the الحنفي school's comprehensive framework for the pilgrimage. Al-Kasani identifies the conditions for the الالتزام of الحج, the pillars (arkan) — ihram, wuquf at Arafah, and tawaf al-ifadah — the necessary acts (wajibat) بما في ذلك sa'y, and the invalida
